explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A third-party guide describes DTE Energy's smart grid upgrade, which incorporates intelligent devices and automation to manage power flow and detect faults. This system implies a governance function for overseeing these AI systems.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party guide to smart grids provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The article describes DTE Energy's smart grid upgrade, which utilizes intelligent devices and automation to manage power flow and detect faults, implying a governance function for overseeing these AI systems. Furthermore, the system's aim to pinpoint outage locations for faster crew response suggests a degree of transparency in how the technology operates and its impact.
relevant_sources.title: A Guide to Smart Grids in 2025

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: A third-party guide describes DTE Energy's smart grid system, noting its aim to pinpoint outage locations for faster crew response. This suggests a degree of transparency regarding how the technology operates and its impact.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party guide to smart grids provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The article describes DTE Energy's smart grid upgrade, which utilizes intelligent devices and automation to manage power flow and detect faults, implying a governance function for overseeing these AI systems. Furthermore, the system's aim to pinpoint outage locations for faster crew response suggests a degree of transparency in how the technology operates and its impact.
relevant_sources.title: A Guide to Smart Grids in 2025

summary.overall_findings: DTE Energy's smart grid upgrade, which utilizes intelligent devices and automation to manage power flow and detect faults, implies a governance function for overseeing these AI systems. Third-party guides also suggest a degree of transparency in how its smart grid technology operates and its impact, specifically its aim to pinpoint outage locations for faster crew response; these two pillars are the only ones with documented evidence out of seven evaluated. Drawing from 9 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
